name: Porriño Return
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Mos traverse diverse landscapes characterized by lush green scenery and scenic valleys. The region is situated near the Serra d'Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park, featuring rolling hills, limestone cliffs, and rich forests. Some routes may include mountain tunnels, adding an element of adventure. Panoramic views of the natural beauty are available from various points along the trails. Best touring cycling routes around Mos The most popular touring cycling route is Porriño Return, a 36.9 miles (59.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 56 minutes to complete, offering a challenging ride through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Waterfall in Teis – View of Ponte de Rande loop from Louredo - Os Valos, a moderate 17.4 miles (28.0 km) path that provides scenic views of a waterfall and the Ponte de Rande. Local touring cyclists also love the Roman Bridge of Orbenlle – Valença Fortress  loop from O Porriño, a 45.0 miles (72.4 km) trail leading through historic sites and natural landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Mos is defined by lush landscapes, rolling hills, and historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult journeys. The routes in Mos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Mos?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Mos, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The region is known for its lush green scenery and diverse landscapes, including rolling hills and forests near the Serra d'Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Mos?
A: The touring cycling routes around Mos cater to various abilities. While there are no easy routes listed, you'll find 6 moderate routes and 19 difficult routes. This range ensures options for both those seeking a scenic, less strenuous ride and those looking for a challenging adventure through varied terrain.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Mos?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes around Mos are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33736779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Teis – View of Ponte de Rande loop from Louredo - Os Valos&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Orbenlle – Valença Fortress 🏰 loop from O Porriño&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes are great for exploring without needing a return trip arrangement.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on touring cycling routes in Mos?
A: Mos's touring cycling routes showcase diverse natural beauty. You can expect lush green scenery, scenic valleys, and the rolling hills and limestone cliffs of the Serra d'Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33736779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Teis – View of Ponte de Rande loop from Louredo - Os Valos&lt;/a&gt;, even feature waterfalls and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes near Mos?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Mos offers several routes that cover significant distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Orbenlle – Valença Fortress 🏰 loop from O Porriño&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 45.0-mile (72.4 km) trail, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952589749/porrino-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porriño Return&lt;/a&gt; covers 36.9 miles (59.3 km), providing ample opportunity for extended touring.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore along the touring cycling routes?
A: Mos's routes often combine natural beauty with historical interest. You can cycle past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/112074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Orbenlle&lt;/a&gt;, or explore routes that lead to the Valença Fortress. The region's proximity to historic towns and sites, as mentioned in the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40371014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Orbenlle – Valença Fortress 🏰 loop from O Porriño&lt;/a&gt;, offers a rich cultural experience alongside your ride.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Mos?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from lush valleys to rolling hills, and the combination of challenging terrain with scenic views. The opportunity to explore historic sites and natural parks is also a highlight for many.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or scenic spots along the routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several viewpoints that provide expansive vistas. The Serra d'Aire e Candeeiros Natural Park, which borders Mos, is known for its panoramic views. While specific viewpoints on the routes are not detailed, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33736779" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Teis – View of Ponte de Rande loop from Louredo - Os Valos&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views of the Ponte de Rande, indicating picturesque sections along the trails.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the touring cycling routes in Mos?
A: While specific cafes or pubs are not listed for each route, the touring cycling routes around Mos often pass through quaint villages and historic towns. These settlements typically offer opportunities to find local eateries, cafes, or shops where you can refuel and experience local culture during your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mos?
A: Mos generally offers favorable conditions for touring cycling due to its mild climate. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be warm, while winters are typically mild, making cycling enjoyable for much of the year. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or beginners in Mos?
A: While many routes around Mos are classified as moderate to difficult, the region's 'ecopistas' (eco-friendly and accessible trails) are generally well-suited for leisurely rides and families. These trails often provide a gentler experience, allowing beginners and families to enjoy the scenic beauty at a comfortable pace. Always check the route details for elevation and terrain to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: What are some notable attractions or points of interest near the touring cycling routes?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, Mos is surrounded by interesting attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/112074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Orbenlle&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4490085" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Redondela Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315205" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte da Veiga&lt;/a&gt;. Natural highlights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Aloia&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1471607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonte de Morgadáns&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse sights to complement your cycling adventure.
tips: A nice place to stop for a drink or something to eat.
Traditional old stone church along the Camino de Santiago

Although its origin is quite possibly Roman, the bridge shows many medieval characteristics. The bridge has undergone serious modifications throughout history, especially in the 14th and 15th centuries.

Many legends surround the old bridge. In one, the bridge was the meeting place for a gang of thieves who gathered here to "split" the booties. Another is the story of a group of knights who departed from here to other places to fight against the Moors. Another tells us that seven brothers gathered here (Asunción, Lucía, María de la O, Marta, Amaro, Blas and Cibrán) who decided to preach the word of Christ. Their work of preaching and doing good was so great that when they died, seven mountains grew over their bodies. They are honored by seven chapels in area towns (Pesqueiras, Ganade, Picoña, Ribarteme, Lira, Areas, Guláns).

The Romans left their mark on the area as this was an important road that linked the region with Tui. A magnificent bridge whose first stones were placed on the riverbed by the great Roman architects. 

The bridge underwent great modifications, in the 15th and especially the 17h century due to the conflicts and wars with Portugal.  The bridge was modified to monitor the passage to the lands of Tudenses (Tui). This is evident by the various fortifications on both sides of the bridge, which today are mostly demolished and covered by vegetation. But the most remarkable thing about this area was the location of a fortress that was part of a military network made up of fortified buildings along the Miño River. The rearmaments of its foundations are hidden under hundreds of square meters of nearby vineyards.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of Santa María do Porriño
